title: Unona micrantha
taxonomy: Unona micrantha is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family comprises over 2,000 species, spread across 128 genera. Within the Annonaceae family, Unona micrantha is further classified into the genus Unona, which is a small genus consisting of a few species of flowering plants. The genus Unona is primarily found in tropical Africa, with some species also distributed in Asia. As a member of the Annonaceae family, Unona micrantha is closely related to other plants in this family, such as the custard apple and the soursop.
uses: Unona micrantha, traditionally used in African folk medicine, has been reported to exhibit various medicinal properties.
